feed.title: SAP Community - Sustainability
feed.subtitle: Sustainability blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Administering Sustainability Control Tower
entry.content:
Why Administration Matters

Before organizations can effectively manage sustainability data, they need a solid technical foundation. SAP Sustainability Control Tower requires proper setup, role management, and system integration to ensure accurate, consistent, and scalable operations.

A well-administered system enables:

  • Reliable data integration across SAP landscapes
  • Secure and structured user access
  • Smooth operation for business users
  • A strong foundation for reporting and steering sustainability

What You’ll Learn in This Learning Journey

This compact, 1.5-hour course focuses on the essential administrative tasks required to get SAP Sustainability Control Tower up and running:

  1. Setting Up Accounts and Services
    Learn how to manage accounts and services to prepare the system for use.
  2. Assigning Roles
    Understand different user roles and how to assign them to ensure proper access control and governance.
  3. Configuring System Integration
    Connect SAP Sustainability Control Tower with key systems, including:
  • SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • SAP Datasphere
  • SAP EHS Management
  • SAP Sustainability Footprint Management
  • SAP Analytics Cloud

This ensures seamless data exchange and a fully connected sustainability landscape.

Prerequisite: Basic knowledge from the Learning Journey Reporting and Steering Sustainability in Your Businesses using SAP Sustainability Control Tower.

Who Should Take the Course?

This learning journey is designed for:

  • System Administrators responsible for setup and configuration

Build the technical foundation for effective sustainability management and start the Learning Journey “Administering SAP Sustainability Control Tower” here.

entry.title: How to Get Started with SAP Sustainability Data Exchange
entry.content:
Why SAP Sustainability Data Exchange Matters

Sustainability doesn’t stop at company boundaries — it extends across the entire supply chain. To truly reduce environmental impact, organizations must collaborate and exchange reliable data with their partners.

SAP Sustainability Data Exchange enables businesses to securely share product carbon footprint data, improving transparency and accelerating decarbonization efforts.

With SAP Sustainability Data Exchange, companies can:

  • Collaborate with partners on sustainability data
  • Increase transparency across the supply chain
  • Standardize and share product carbon footprint information
  • Support data-driven decarbonization initiatives

What You’ll Learn in This Learning Journey

This short, 1-hour 22-minute course introduces the key concepts and practical steps to start using SAP Sustainability Data Exchange:

  1. Introduction and Business Challenges
    Understand supply chain sustainability challenges and how SAP Sustainability Data Exchange enables collaboration between business partners.
  2. Leveraging Networks
    Learn how the solution integrates with networks like:
  • SAP Business Network
  • Catena-X
  1. Subscription and Setup
    Learn how to provision and adapt  SAP Sustainability Data Exchange within your SAP Business Technology Platform environment.
  2. Getting Started with Data Exchange

      Learn how to:

  • Maintain master data for products and business partners
  • Exchange product carbon footprint data
  • Understand end-to-end carbon management scenarios with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management

Who Should Take the Course?

This learning journey is ideal for:

  • Consultants supporting sustainability and SAP implementations

No prerequisites are required, making it a great starting point for beginners.

Start collaborating on supply chain sustainability today and explore the Learning Journey “Getting Started with SAP Sustainability Data Exchange” here.

entry.title: How to Set Up SAP Sustainability Data Exchange
entry.content:
Why Setup and Integration Matter

To enable effective data exchange across the supply chain, systems must be properly connected. SAP Sustainability Data Exchange relies on strong integrations to ensure that product carbon footprint data flows securely and consistently between partners and platforms.

A well-configured setup enables:

  • Accurate and automated data exchange
  • Reliable integration across sustainability solutions
  • Scalable collaboration with business partners
  • Consistent and trustworthy sustainability reporting

What You’ll Learn in This Learning Journey

This short, 16-minute course focuses on the essential integration setup for SAP Sustainability Data Exchange:

  1. Integration Capabilities and Setup
    Learn how to configure technical integrations between SAP Sustainability Data Exchange and key systems, including:
  • SAP Sustainability Footprint Management
  • Catena-X 

This ensures seamless data exchange and accurate sustainability reporting across your ecosystem.

Who Should Take the Course?

This learning journey is designed for:

  • Consultants supporting sustainability and integration scenarios
  • Administrators responsible for system setup and connectivity

Prerequisite: Basic knowledge from the Learning Journey Getting Started with SAP Sustainability Data Exchange.

Enable seamless sustainability data exchange and start the Learning Journey “Setting Up SAP Sustainability Data Exchange” here.

entry.title: How to Establish Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with SAP
entry.content:
Why Carbon Footprint Management Matters

Measuring emissions is essential for any sustainability strategy. Without accurate data, organizations cannot effectively reduce their environmental impact or meet regulatory requirements.

SAP Sustainability Footprint Management enables companies to calculate carbon emissions across their operations, from individual activities to the entire corporate footprint.

With this solution, businesses can:

  • Calculate emissions across GHG Scope 1, 2, and 3
  • Gain transparency into their environmental impact
  • Support regulatory reporting and compliance
  • Build a foundation for decarbonization initiatives

What You’ll Learn in This Learning Journey

This two-hour course provides a comprehensive introduction to corporate carbon footprint management:

GHG Protocol and Regulations

Understand the fundamentals of the Greenhouse Gas Protocol and key regulatory frameworks.

Introduction to SAP Sustainability Footprint Management

Explore the features, capabilities, and architecture of the solution and how it supports carbon tracking across products, operations, and value chains.

System Setup and Configuration

Learn how to set up SAP Sustainability Footprint Management in your SAP Business Technology Platform environment.

Managing Emissions Data

Maintain source data and prepare it for accurate emissions calculation.

Calculating Carbon Footprints

Calculate emissions across Scope 1, Scope 2, and Scope 3 and analyze the results.

Integrating with Corporate Steering

Share calculated emissions data with SAP Sustainability Control Tower to support reporting and strategic decision-making.

Who Should Take the Course?

This learning journey is ideal for:

  • Business Users working with sustainability and reporting
  • Consultants implementing carbon management solutions
  • Administrators responsible for setup and configuration

No prerequisites are required, making it suitable for beginners.

Start building a solid foundation for carbon management and explore the Learning Journey “Establishing Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with Simple Configuration” here.

entry.title: What an event! Where else can such a community come together?!
entry.content:
Last week, from March 24–25, the SAP EHS & Product Compliance community met in person in Amsterdam at the SAP EHS & Product Compliance Conference hosted by TAC Insights. The atmosphere was fantastic, and the networking within the community was incredibly lively!

For many, the event began already on Monday evening with partner and customer dinners, as well as preparations for the conference and the partner exhibition hall.

On Tuesday morning, with 220 attendees, the main conference room was packed for our opening keynote. SAP’s Gunther Rothermel shared more about the new generation of agentic processes for operational sustainability—also highlighted in his LinkedIn post.

The agenda continued in this manner, featuring experts who covered EHS and Product Compliance capabilities and innovations, including insights from experienced customers, partners, and SAP specialists.

Across both days, we heard real-world implementation stories, with customers sharing their S/4HANA transformation journeys within the dedicated EHS and Product Compliance tracks. Particularly fascinating were the side-by-side and hybrid approaches customers are adopting to kick-start their transformations instead of waiting for a “perfect moment.” Another strong theme that came through was the added value our partners bring to customer projects by filling gaps and extending what’s possible with our solutions.

As expected, AI was a major recurring topic—not just in terms of future potential, but with concrete demos from SAP and partners. Attendees got a glimpse of how AI can support their everyday work. This theme also shaped a panel discussion on how AI will influence future EHS and Product Compliance processes.

Another highlight was the Discovery Hub. Attendees had the chance to get hands-on experience with the solutions, following trial tours through dangerous goods and compliance document management processes in the Product Compliance room, as well as AI-assisted compliance management and AI-powered Safety Instruction Generation for Risk Assessment in the EHS room. Our SAP experts were kept very busy supporting visitors and discussing scenarios in depth.

Whether during breaks in the exhibition hall at partner or SAP booths, at the evening BBQ, or in the interactive sessions throughout the days—there was always something happening. Peers had countless opportunities to exchange ideas on topics affecting their day-to-day work. Despite the professional focus, the atmosphere remained friendly, honest and open. I’m already looking forward to seeing everyone again!

And that brings me to what’s coming next. Please save the dates:

  • SAP EHS & Product Compliance Virtual Infodays
    September 29 – October 1, 2026
    More information and invitations will follow soon.
  • SAP EHS & Product Compliance Conference presented by TAC Insights
    March 23–24, 2027 in Berlin, Germany
    Visit the event page for details.

 If you’d like to hear more or are interested in contributing to any of the above, feel free to contact us at product.compliance@sap.com.

entry.title: Agentic AI at the 2026 SAP EHS and Product Compliance Conference
entry.content:
On March 24 and 25, TAC Insights and SAP hosted the SAP EHS and Product Compliance Conference in Amsterdam. In a great atmosphere, the SAP EHS and Product Compliance community met in person and benefitted from a full agenda and various networking opportunities. For more information, see this blog post.

One hot topic at the conference was agentic AI and its use in EHS and Product Compliance processes. Agentic AI refers to autonomous systems that act as agents. These make decisions and execute multi-step tasks to achieve specific goals without constant human guidance.

In his conference keynote, Gunther Rothermel, Chief Product Officer and Co-General Manager for SAP Sustainability, explained SAP's product strategy for sustainability. He also described how embedded AI can drive efficiency and reduce operational risks across the product lifecycle. SAP is developing AI agents for EHS and Product Compliance. The EHS Workplace Safety Agent transforms safety observations into continuously mitigated risks and up-to-date safety instructions. It uncovers overlooked hazards, enhances risk assessments, and recommends corrective and preventive actions. The agent automatically generates or updates safety instructions for a safer, more compliant workplace. In Product Compliance, the planned GHS Classification and Labeling Agent supports and accelerates the process of classifying and labeling products based on the Globally Harmonized System (GHS). The agent is based on the latest regulations, reduces manual workload, and minimizes errors. Through seamless integration into the product compliance solution, the agent determines and collects the required input data. It then calculates and proposes the GHS classification and label elements for use in subsequent product compliance processes. The AI agent checks for data gaps and validates data against regulations to ensure accuracy. Users can review the results using explanations of how the agent reached its conclusions.

In a panel discussion, Gunther Rothermel and two SAP customers discussed the real-world impact of AI in EHS and Product Compliance processes. All panelists emphasized the importance of clean and reliable data as a foundation for AI. AI was also part of several customer and partner presentations. These presentations included, for example, the role of AI in safety data sheet authoring. In the Discovery Hub room, attendees could get hands-on experience with the SAP solutions for EHS and Product Compliance. In the EHS room, participants could, for example, try out the AI-assisted compliance management and AI-powered safety instruction generation for risk assessments.

Toward the end of the conference, participants discussed how agentic AI could support or change their work in a lively roundtable. Some SAP customers said that they already use agentic AI to propose potential substance substitutions, explain classifications through a Hazard Communication Chatbot, and support recipe development. One customer expressed concerns that the use of AI is restricted in European countries due to labor laws. The customer also noted that they don't want to share their formulations in the cloud, for example, for AI-based classifications.

When asked about ideas and wishes for use cases supported by agentic AI, customers discussed the following:

  • AI-supported analytics could provide implications for the whole product portfolio after a regulatory change. Going one step further, AI could propose potential future regulatory changes and their potential impact on the product portfolio.
  • AI could simulate compliance before a product is released.
  • Some sustainability requirements are vague, complicated, and data is often missing. AI could propose values for missing data. In general, AI could help identify data gaps and clean up data.
  • AI could help analyze customer requests.
  • Safety data sheets contain a lot of information and are provided in various formats. AI could extract the key information.
  • SAP’s new Product Compliance solution uses a different data model than the classic solution. For the migration from the classic to the new solution, data needs to be mapped. AI could support this mapping.

What do you think about these use cases? Do you have additional ideas? Provide them as comments on this blog post.

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Footprint Management: Q1-26 Updates & Highlights
entry.content:
As we continue into 2026, I’m back with the next edition of my quarterly blog series covering the latest innovations and highlights in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. Over the last quarter, we’ve extended existing capabilities, introduced new ones, and invested further in usability, integration, and enablement – all with the goal of helping our customers calculate, analyze, and operationalize sustainability footprints at scale, grounded in ERP data. In this edition, I’m covering:

  • Transport Footprint Enhancements
  • Customer‑Relevant Transport Footprints (Scope 3.9)
  • Distance Adjustment Factors
  • Transport Analytics
  • Categorize Scope 3.2 Footprints
  • Usability Improvements
  • Other Release Highlights
  • Unibloom Emission Factor Datasets
  • Multi‑Impact Footprints in Practice: Learning Journey, FLAG and TfS Use Cases
  • Additional Information

Transport Footprint Enhancements

The last quarter brought several enhancements to the ERP‑centric transport footprint calculation capabilities in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. These updates extend coverage to customer-relevant transport footprints, including integration of customer master data, give you more control over the distance calculations used for transport footprints, and provide you with comprehensive analytical capabilities.

Customer‑Relevant Transport Footprints (Scope 3.9)

In one of my previous posts, I introduced ERP‑centric transport footprint calculation for upstream and internal transport, covering Greenhouse Gas Protocol Scope 3.4 emissions. This capability has now been extended to the downstream side of the value chain, enabling the calculation of customer‑relevant transport footprints and, with that, Scope 3.9 (Downstream Transport and Distribution) emissions.

The calculation also leverages existing ERP business transaction data and builds on the same transport modeling approach used for upstream transport. The foundation of this enhancement is the introduction of a Customer role for business partners. Business partners with this role, including address data, can be replicated directly from integrated SAP S/4HANA source systems, through APIs, or imported via file in the Import Master Data app. The system derives customer‑relevant transport activities from the following business transactions:

  • Goods Issue for Customer (Outbound deliveries of products to customers)
  • Customer Return (Goods returned by customers to company locations).

When business transactions are replicated from integrated source systems, these transactions now include the Customer ID, ensuring correct assignment of customer‑relevant transport activities during transport footprint calculation. In this context, targeted enhancements were made to the Import Master Data and Import Business Transactions OData APIs on SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Based on the configurations in the Manage Transport Settings app, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management automatically generates transport activities, determines routes and legs, and calculates footprints per activity, route, and leg, applying the relevant emission factors by mode of transport. The resulting Scope 3.9 emissions are seamlessly integrated into both Corporate Carbon Footprint (CCF) and Product Carbon Footprint (PCF) calculations and are available in the standard footprint result and analytical apps – all grounded in ERP data rather than manual estimates.

For setup and process details, see Calculating Customer‑Relevant Transport Footprints Using Business Transactions in SAP Help. For importing business partners and business transactions, check Configurations for Importing Master Data and Configurations for Importing Business Transaction Data.

Customer Relevant Transport Footprints (Scope 3.9)

Distance Adjustment Factors

Transport footprint calculations in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management derive distances between locations automatically using the Great Circle Distance (GCD) method. Since real transport routes often deviate from the shortest path due to road networks, infrastructure, and routing constraints, GCD can underestimate the actual distance traveled. To reflect this, you can now configure distance adjustment factors for different modes of transport in the Manage Transport Settings app. When routes and legs are generated, the system applies the configured adjustment factor to the GCD, resulting in a more realistic distance basis for footprint calculations.

Before configuring distance adjustment factors, you should review the methodology documentation of your emission factor sources. Some emission factor datasets already include distance adjustments – for example, GLEC v3.1 emission factors for air transport. Applying an additional adjustment factor in such cases would lead to incorrect results.

Also note that existing transport activities aren’t updated automatically. To apply new distance adjustment factors, routes and legs must be regenerated and the relevant transport footprints recalculated. For details, check SAP Help: Configuring Distance Adjustment Factors and Generating Routes and Legs.

Configure Distance Adjustment Factors

Transport Analytics

To complement the transport footprint calculation capabilities, a new analytics app called Transport is now available in the Analytics section of the SAP Fiori launchpad. The app provides a comprehensive, map‑based view of corporate and product transport footprints for a selected footprint inventory scope and period. Transport legs are visualized as connections between source and target locations, with color‑coding that highlights higher‑emission legs. You can filter by product, mode of transport, business transaction type, or location, and switch to a table view for detailed, leg‑level analysis sorted by emission value. The app also supports comparisons across different periods. To use the app, you need the SUS_PFM_Calculate_Transport_Footprints_Read role. See SAP Help for more information: Analyzing Footprints for Transport.

Transport Analytics

To reflect all those updates, the process steps for calculating transport footprints using business transaction data have also been updated in the Scenario Guide: Process 8 – Calculating Scope 3.4 and Scope 3.9 Emissions Using Business Transaction Data.

 

Categorize Scope 3.2 Footprints

Until now, all purchased product footprints covered in the Manage Purchased Product Footprints app were automatically attributed to GHG Protocol Scope 3.1 (Purchased Goods and Services) in corporate carbon footprint calculations. For most companies, this is the correct assignment – but not in all cases. Capital goods such as machinery, equipment, or buildings that are purchased and used over multiple years belong to Scope 3.2 (Capital Goods). Correctly distinguishing between Scope 3.1 and Scope 3.2 is important both for GHG Protocol compliance and for maintaining a high‑quality footprint inventory. This is also a feature that was requested by customers via SAP Influence.

With a recent enhancement, you can now explicitly assign a purchased product footprint or product group footprint to either:

  • Scope 3.1 – Purchased Goods and Services, or
  • Scope 3.2 – Capital Goods

This categorization is supported consistently across all relevant workflows in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management:

  • When creating purchased product footprints manually,
  • When mapping emission factors manually or using AI‑based mapping, and
  • When importing purchased product footprints via data files.

Scope 3.2 Categorization

This enhancement ensures that each purchased product footprint is correctly attributed when calculating your organizational footprint inventory, resulting in a more accurate and auditable split between Scope 3.1 and Scope 3.2. Existing purchased product footprints that were previously categorized automatically remain assigned to Scope 3.1 by default, ensuring backward compatibility. For more information, see Creating Footprints for Purchased Products and Product Groups in SAP Help.

For analyzing your calculated footprints, the updated categorization is also reflected correctly in the Footprint Overview app, where purchased product footprints assigned to Scope 3.2 are shown separately from Scope 3.1.

 

Usability Improvements

To support more efficient day‑to‑day work with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, we continuously invest in improving usability and increasing automation. Last quarter, we introduced several enhancements around form‑based data collection, manual data imports, and better transparency in integrations. These improvements focus on reducing manual effort in recurring data collection tasks, simplifying bulk operations, and making changes and corrections easier to manage at scale – especially in scenarios with large numbers of forms, users, or integrated systems. Many of these enhancements are the direct result of customer feedback, collected through SAP Influence as well as ongoing conversations with customers and partners.

You can now change the status of GHG data forms directly during data import when using spreadsheet templates in the Record GHG Data and Manage GHG Data Collections apps. GHG forms can be set to Applicable or Not Applicable as part of the import. When setting a form to Not Applicable, a comment is required, consistent with the behavior in the user interface. This applies to both bulk templates on the main page of the apps and the individual templates per GHG form type, streamlining bulk updates and reducing manual follow‑up steps.

To further automate and improve correction workflows, GHG data forms that were previously set to Completed or Not Applicable can now be reopened via API using the POST/massUpdate action in the GHG Data Forms API on SAP Business Accelerator Hub. This provides additional flexibility for automated correction scenarios and integration workflows where reported data needs to be revised after submission.

For providing a more consistent and efficient user experience, downloaded templates now better reflect the UI and applied filters. Template sorting now mirrors the UI table sorting, and templates can be downloaded together with the currently applied UI filters. These enhancements apply to apps such as Manage Planned Energy Consumption Rates, Manage Business Activities, Model Energy Flows, Manage Waste Footprints, Record GHG Data, Manage Transport Operation Footprints, Manage Allocations, Manage Purchased Product Footprints, and Manage Transport Settings. In the Calculate Footprints, Model Energy Flows, Record GHG Data, and Manage GHG Data Collections apps, downloading templates together with the displayed data is now the default behavior.

Finally, the sustainability data integration apps now include a Change History tab in Manage Replications, Manage Source Systems, and Configure Extensions for Replications. This allows you to track who made which changes and when, including the old and new values for edited fields, improving transparency and traceability when managing replication configurations.

 

Other Release Highlights

When performing a current period calculation, input items for footprint items of type Product that only contribute a defined CO₂e value without a product quantity are no longer considered in the per‑unit calculation. These input items are excluded because they have no associated product quantity and would otherwise distort per‑unit results, for example transports or allocations. Their CO₂e values are still fully aggregated into the total footprint. When using CO₂e‑only input items, make sure they’re scaled to the total product quantity where appropriate.

The system now supports goods receipt from production even without production history such as goods issues to production, work‑in‑progress postings, or internal service confirmations in the current period. In these cases, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management uses the previous period footprint per unit of the produced product or, if not available, maintained purchased product footprints. This eliminates previous calculation errors and applies the product footprint determination methods defined in the calculation variant.

Custom temperature conditions can now be replicated from source systems using the Manage Replications app. This applies to SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition source systems. For pull‑based replication scenarios, the setup requires custom CDS views in the source system.

Lastly, we’re also continuously enhancing our documentation on SAP Help to improve transparency and onboarding. The Implement – Getting Started section now includes a link to ERP Objects, providing details on relevant CDS views, tables, and fields in SAP S/4HANA for each supported master data entity. In addition, the Product Carbon Footprint Scenario Guide has been extended with step‑by‑step processes for calculating product carbon footprints for cradle‑to‑gate and cradle‑to‑grave scenarios, including concrete examples for allocating Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions using business activities, allocations, and business transaction data.

 

Unibloom Emission Factor Datasets

One of the persistent challenges in Scope 3 reporting is the gap between supplier‑specific data and the global average emission factors that many companies fall back on when that data isn’t available. Those averages can be misleading. For agricultural commodities, for instance, emission factors can vary by up to 200% depending on the country of origin, reflecting differences in land‑use change, energy consumption, and local production practices. The wider the gap between your actual supply chain and the averages you apply, the less reliable your footprint calculations become. To overcome this challenge, we onboarded a new addition to the SAP partner ecosystem: Unibloom. Unibloom’s country‑specific supply‑chain emissions datasets are now available on SAP Store and can be integrated directly with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management.

Unibloom provides ISO 14044‑aligned, country‑specific emission factors for agricultural and industrial commodities, including land‑use‑ and management‑adjusted data. The coverage spans key sectors such as SBTi FLAG (Forest, Land, and Agriculture). The datasets are delivered in a ready‑to‑import format and can be extended on demand with additional country‑specific factors for specific materials, ingredients, or industries. With Unibloom datasets in place, you can replace broad global averages with granular, location‑specific emission factors in your footprint calculations. This improves accuracy and auditability, increases confidence in reported results, and supports more informed sourcing and procurement decisions.

To go deeper into the datasets and their practical use with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, we’re hosting a joint webinar with Unibloom. The session focuses on improving Scope 3 accuracy for agricultural and FLAG‑relevant value chains and shows how country‑specific emission factors can be applied in practice. Access the webinar and register here: End data paralysis. Know when your Scope 3 data is good enough.

 

Multi‑Impact Footprints in Practice: Learning Journey, FLAG and TfS Use Cases

To support our customers in moving beyond pure carbon metrics, we introduced multi‑impact environmental footprints in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management last quarter – I covered the core capability in more detail in my deep dive.

To help you apply this capability in practice, consistently across different reporting frameworks and industry requirements, we’ve invested in further enablement resources. As a starting point, a new Learning Journey provides a structured introduction to the topic: Establishing the Multi-impact Environmental Footprint with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. It walks through the underlying concepts, configuration, and usage of multi‑impact footprints, providing the context for how additional environmental impact categories can be calculated, managed, and analyzed alongside carbon, using a single, consistent data model.

With the basics in place, the focus is now shifting from how multi‑impact categories work to how they can be applied in practice for concrete reporting frameworks and industry requirements. In this context, my colleague @Cielo_SanabriaRuiz has published two follow‑up posts that demonstrate exactly that:

The first, SBTi Forest, Land, and Agriculture (FLAG) in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, explains why distinguishing land‑based emissions from fossil emissions matters at both corporate and product level. It walks through the specific impact categories and emission factors required for SBTi FLAG compliance and includes a mapping table that links impact categories to FLAG reporting categories.

The second, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management for the Chemicals (Together for Sustainability), focuses on the chemical industry and the Together for Sustainability (TfS) guideline. It covers the sector‑specific setup for product carbon footprints and Scope 3.1 calculations, including allocation rules for multi‑output processes, transport requirements such as the Radiative Forcing Index (RFI) for air transport, and a complete mapping of impact categories to TfS reporting standards.

Taken together, this enablement material shows how multi‑impact footprints can be used to support FLAG and TfS reporting requirements without fragmenting data models or calculation logic, while still staying aligned with established standards. If you’re working on FLAG reporting, chemical‑industry footprinting, or more broadly on extending footprint calculations beyond carbon, these resources provide a coherent path from concepts to practical application.

 

Additional Information

For in-depth details on SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, including concepts, setup, and the application help, I recommend you browsing through the SAP Help Portal. For an overview of all new features and functions that have been released, check the What’s New section, which is updated with every new software release. To continue receiving e-mail notifications, you need to subscribe again – just visit the page and choose Subscribe.

For exploring and experimenting in a live system, register for the free SAP Sustainability Footprint Management Trial. You’ll receive an e-mail with access information within seconds after your registration and can then test the solution at your own convenience within a 30-day trial period. It also includes a wide range of guided tours designed to help you navigate through all the features and functions comprehensively. Recently, we also added new tours and will shortly release additional ones, representing the latest features and an enhanced user experience.

To stay up to date on SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, follow me here in the SAP Community. In regular cadence, I’ll publish blog posts giving you updates on all the product’s new features and functions as well as deep dives into the product capabilities. I’m also looking forward to engaging with you directly, feel free to leave your feedback in the comment section.

entry.title: What’s New in Q1-2026? SAP Sustainability Footprint Management Updates
entry.content:
To stay ahead in the world of ESG, your tools need to evolve as fast as the regulations do. SAP has just released the Q1-2026 highlights for SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, and there are some game-changing features for anyone managing carbon data.

The latest updates focus on precision, automation, and expanding the scope of audit-ready calculations.

Key Highlights from Q1-2026: 🚛 Enhanced Transport Footprints: New capabilities for customer-relevant transport emissions, including better distance control and route automation. 📊 New Analytics App: A dedicated "Transport" analytics app providing a map-based view of your corporate and product footprints. 🎯 Granular Scope 3 Reporting: Improved attribution for purchased product footprints, ensuring a more accurate split between Scope 3.1 and 3.2. 🤖 Increased Automation: New usability enhancements in data collection forms and manual imports to reduce the "manual heavy lifting" of ESG data.

Whether you are deep into implementation or just planning your roadmap, these updates show exactly how SAP is turning sustainability from a reporting burden into a strategic advantage.

👉 I highly recommend checking out the full article for all the details: https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-sustainability-footprint-management-q1-26-updates-amp-highlights/ba-p/14363686

#SAP #Sustainability #FootprintManagement #Q12026 #CarbonAccounting #ESGUpdates #SupplyChain #NetZero #SAPCommunity

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Academy Newsletter April 2026
entry.content:
Dear customers,
Find here a preview of the SAP Enterprise Support Academy monthly newsletter on latest news, training courses, upcoming sessions, and further educational offerings.
The SAP Enterprise Support Academy provides expert guidance and high impact knowledge transfer services that enable you to innovate and be successful with your SAP solutions.

Subscribe to regularly receive latest news and stay informed about the wide range of educational offerings provided by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy that help you to build the digital skills tailored to your needs.

Ensuring a Smooth SAP Datasphere Deployment

Preparing to deploy SAP Datasphere as part of SAP Business Data Cloud? The continuous quality check (CQC) for deployment readiness helps identify configuration risks early and ensures that your setup aligns with SAP Best Practices before go-live.

Start today

SAP Customer Experience Value Packages

Connect common customer experience challenges with the capabilities available in SAP Customer Experience solutions. Learn how Value Packages help turn CX goals into practical improvements.

Link to SAP Community

Sustainability with SAP 

Ever wondered how to make sustainability a part of your daily business decisions? SAP's new platform helps you track, report, and act on your environmental impact without overhauling your existing processes.

SAP Sustainability - SAP Community

SAP Business Network: 2026 Q1 Highlights & What’s Next

Get a quick look at how SAP Business Network is evolving with new compliance, AI, and process updates. Explore what’s changing and what’s coming next to keep your operations moving forward.

Explore

Conversion to SAP S/4HANA Finance – Deep Dive

Join us in a multiday training session to discover everything you need to know about all phases and activities involved in Conversion of Accounting to SAP S/4HANA. 

EGI: Conversion of Accounting to SAP S/4HANA

Spend Management Webcast Series

Discover how SAP’s Spend Management Webcast Series helps you learn at your own pace through flexible live and on demand sessions. Explore practical topics that fit easily into your day.

Spend Management webcast series

Your Transition to Next-gen SAP Ariba

Your transition to next-gen SAP Ariba starts here—find the essentials, stay updated, and explore what the new platform means for your everyday work.

Explore more

How to Get Started With SAP S/4HANA Finance

Are you about to start with SAP S/4HANA Finance? Then don’t miss our interactive virtual Mee-the-Expert live session that helps you get on board.

Register through this blog post: Get started with SAP S/4HANA Finance

SAP S/4HANA Finance Fundamentals – Deep Dive

Join us in a multiday training session to discover everything you need to know about benefits and added capabilities of SAP Finance in SAP S/4HANA. Get empowered to build and evaluate the business case for your digital transformation 

EGI: SAP S/4HANA Finance: Fundamentals

Conversion to SAP S/4HANA Finance – Deep Dive

Join us in a multiday training session to discover everything you need to know about all phases and activities involved in Conversion of Accounting to SAP S/4HANA. 

EGI: Conversion of Accounting to SAP S/4HANA

Ready to Transform Your Finance Operations?

Optimize your project experience with Finance in SAP Cloud ERP and our e-learning course. Streamline workflows, automate processes, and unlock real-time financial insights. Master general ledger, asset accounting, and cash management. Perfect for finance professionals!

Start today

Master SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Implementation

Accelerate your project success with online training on SAP Activate, RISE Methodology, and proven implementation strategies. Learn essential tools, extensibility capabilities, and integration techniques to streamline your SAP Cloud ERP deployment. 

Enroll now

Accelerate Your SAP S/4HANA Success

Discover how to use the right tools to cut your project timeline and maximize ROI. Learn when and how to leverage SAP Readiness Check, Road Map Viewer, ABAP Test Cockpit, and more. Master the essential strategies for a faster time-to-value journey

Enroll our free e-learning course today

Reducing Trade Regulation Penalties with SAP Global Trade Services

Discover how SAP Global Trade Services automates your foreign-trade processes, ensures regulatory compliance, and reduces costly penalties. Learn to streamline customs clearance, minimize manual errors, and optimize your global supply chain—all in one comprehensive e-learning course.

Join now and transform your trade operations

Unlock Transformational Value with SAP Services & Support

Join us on May 11 for the ASUG Pre-Conference and an exclusive showfloor tour at Sapphire Orlando. This full‑day session introduces SAP’s modernized Services & Support portfolio and the tiered engagement model — Foundational, Advanced, and Max — designed to help organizations accelerate cloud adoption, boost productivity with embedded AI, and achieve predictable outcomes.

 

Discover essential portfolio components such as Development Services (with AI co-innovation), Application Management, and SAP Sovereign Cloud, and learn how they enhance operations, user experience, and innovation. Whether preparing for cloud migration or integrating AI, you’ll gain clear, actionable guidance to maximize value from your SAP investment.

May 11, 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM
Showfloor Tour: 4:30 PM – 6 PM

Seats are limited—register now

Unlock Revenue Growth Through Smarter Product Configuration

Join our e-learning course and master SAP Variant Configuration, Pricing & Intelligent Product Recommendations. Learn to deliver personalized customer experiences, accelerate quote-to-close cycles, and boost revenue with AI-driven insights. Transform how you configure, price, and recommend products across your entire ecosystem.

Enroll now and elevate your expertise

New: Choosing partners with trusted support expertise in SAP Partner Finder

SAP has introduced a new support proficiency designation for partners in SAP Partner Finder, which empowers customers to choose partners with trusted support expertise. It recognizes partners with established capabilities for minimizing delays and preventing project risks. 

Details here

Be future-ready with SAP Services and Support

Watch this webinar to see how the SAP Services and Support portfolio is evolving to deliver a simpler, more powerful customer experience.

Watch now

 

Related links:

  • SAP Enterprise Support Academy on LSC (learning.sap.com)
  • SAP Support Portal
  • SAP Enterprise Support value maps 
  • Video

entry.title: Establishing Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with Advanced Configurations
entry.content:
Why Advanced Carbon Management Matters

As sustainability requirements grow, basic carbon calculations are no longer enough. Organizations need deeper insights into their emissions across operations, supply chains, and product lifecycles.

SAP Sustainability Footprint Management enables advanced carbon footprint calculations by integrating business data, applying intelligent mapping, and analysing emissions across all relevant scopes.

With advanced capabilities, businesses can:

  • Calculate emissions across complex Scope 1, 2, and 3 scenarios
  • Integrate data from ERP and external systems
  • Leverage AI for emission factor mapping
  • Identify emission hotspots and reduction opportunities
  • Drive targeted decarbonization strategies

What You’ll Learn in This Learning Journey

This in-depth, 3-hour course builds on foundational knowledge and introduces advanced carbon management capabilities:

  1. System Integration
    Learn how to integrate SAP Sustainability Footprint Management with ERP systems to access relevant business data.
  2. Energy Flow Modelling
    Model energy consumption and calculate emissions for Scope 1, Scope 2, and selected Scope 3 categories using advanced energy flow logic.
  3. Supplier and Purchased Goods Emissions (Scope 3.1)
    Use AI-based mapping and integrate supplier data, including via SAP Sustainability Data Exchange, to calculate emissions from purchased goods and services.
  4. Transport Emissions (Scope 3.4)
    Configure and calculate emissions related to logistics and transportation activities.
  5. Waste Emissions (Scope 3.5)
    Track and calculate emissions resulting from waste management processes.
  6. Product Use Emissions (Scope 3.11)
    Assess emissions generated during the use phase of sold products and allocate them correctly.
  7. Financial Integration
    Publish carbon data to SAP Green Ledger for financial and reporting alignment.
  8. Identifying Hotspots
    Analyse emissions data to identify key drivers and uncover actionable decarbonization levers.

Who Should Take the Course?

This learning journey is ideal for:

  • Data Analysts working with sustainability data
  • Consultants implementing advanced carbon management solutions
  • Business Users analyzing emissions and performance
  • Administrators managing configuration and integration

Prerequisite: Knowledge from the Learning Journey Establishing Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with Simple Configuration.

Take your carbon management to the next level and explore the Learning Journey “Establishing Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with Advanced Configuration” here.

entry.title: In Brief - Product Compliance Dangerous Goods Management S/4HANA - End to End Overview
entry.content:
In Brief - Product Compliance Dangerous Goods Management - End to End Overview

Transporting dangerous goods is subject to strict regulatory requirements and increasing scrutiny across global supply chains. Non compliance can lead to serious safety risks, legal consequences, and operational disruptions.

At the same time, many organizations still rely on fragmented processes, manual checks, and inconsistent data, making it difficult to ensure compliance at scale and across functions.

In this video, we illustrate how SAP S/4HANA supports an integrated approach to Dangerous Goods Management as part of Product Compliance. By embedding compliance directly into product master data and connecting it with logistics processes, companies can ensure that relevant regulations are considered consistently from the very beginning.

This enables automated checks during order and delivery processing, improves data transparency, and helps ensure that all required information, documentation, and handling instructions are available where they are needed.

An integrated approach not only reduces compliance risks but also increases efficiency and supports reliable and safe transport execution across markets.

Watch the demo video to see how this can be realized in practice.

In Brief - Product Compliance Dangerous Goods Management - End to End Overview

entry.title: SAP Green Ledger: Q1-26 Release Updates and Highlights
entry.content:
With this blog post, I would like to introduce a regular quarterly update series highlighting the latest innovations and enhancements in SAP Green Ledger. These updates provide an overview of new features and improvements delivered during the last quarter, helping you stay up to date with ongoing developments.

Short introduction: What is SAP Green Ledger? SAP Green Ledger supports organizations in integrating carbon into financial processes. It runs as an application on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) and integrates with SAP S/4HANA Cloud–based finance systems. It enables organizations to monitor and document carbon emission impacts at transaction level and link emissions to financial objects. By using established financial structures such as accounts, company codes, and controlling objects, carbon emissions can be consistently assigned to business activities and financial categories. This supports transparent and auditable reporting, as well as data-driven operational and strategic decision-making.

With Q1 2026, SAP Green Ledger continues to evolve, supporting organizations in moving beyond emissions tracking toward managing carbon as a financial dimension. The features released in the first quarter of this year bring a range of enhancements in carbon liability management, regulatory readiness, and financial integration, along with improvements that make everyday processes more transparent and flexible. This blog post covers the following new features:

  • CBAM Functionality: Configuring Carbon Certificate Data and Managing Carbon Liabilities
  • Determining Mandatory Accounts for Carbon Certificates
  • Linking Carbon Data to Projects
  • Supporting Multiple SAP Sustainability Footprint Management Systems
  • Posting on Blocked Accounts
  • Reversing Manual Journal Entries
  • Further Updates and Improvements

 

CBAM Functionality: Configuring Carbon Certificate Data and Managing Carbon Liabilities

One of the key highlights this quarter is the support for carbon certificate data and carbon liabilities. Carbon certificates and liabilities play a central role in regulatory frameworks such as the EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM), where companies must cover embedded emissions with certificates that carry a financial value. Since the EU CBAM came into effect in January 2026, companies that import specific goods into the EU are now required to report embedded emissions in these imported goods and build up corresponding carbon liabilities. While the actual purchase of EU CBAM certificates will only begin in 2027, organizations already need to track and value these obligations today.

To support these new requirements, the new Configure Carbon Certificate Data app provides a centralized entry point for maintaining the data required for carbon certificate handling and valuation. You can create and maintain Emissions Trading System (ETS) prices, which represent the cost of emitting one metric ton of carbon dioxide equivalent (CO2e). Since these prices fluctuate over time and directly impact liability valuation, maintaining them each calendar week helps ensure accuracy and alignment with market developments.

Maintaining Weekly EU ETS Prices (Illustrative Values Only)

In addition, you can display available company codes and access yearly CBAM factors. These factors determine the portion of embedded emissions that must be covered by CBAM certificates. By bringing ETS pricing, company context, and CBAM factors together, the app creates a consistent foundation that connects configuration with carbon data collection, liability calculation, and journal entry processing.

The flow is further supported by enhancements in the Manage Carbon Collections app. When creating a carbon collection, you can now indicate whether carbon certificates are required and maintain relevant information such as the number of certificates to surrender. This ensures that certificate requirements are captured consistently at the point of data entry. The information can then be used in later processing steps.

Carbon Certificate -relevant Carbon Collection

Building on this, the new Manage Carbon Liabilities app provides a dedicated space to view and evaluate carbon liabilities resulting from carbon collections that require certificates. You can analyze both the number of certificates to surrender as well as their corresponding monetary value based on ETS prices, reflecting the financial obligations associated with embedded emissions.

A key part of this process is fair value calculation. The system now calculates the fair value of carbon liabilities automatically for the current calendar year. This calculation is triggered whenever ETS prices are created, updated, or deleted, as well as when carbon collections requiring certificates are posted to the journal. Fair values are calculated by multiplying the number of certificates to surrender by the latest quarterly average ETS price. The calculated fair value covers the period from January 1 to the selected posting date within the year. At the same time, you can still trigger fair value calculations manually. This allows you to recalculate values when needed while keeping posting logic consistent.

Transparency is further improved through integration with journal entries as in the Manage Journal Entries app, you can review data originating from carbon collections. This includes, next to the line items for the collected GHG emissions, CBAM embedded emissions and certificate quantities now.

Journal Entries for CBAM-relevant Carbon Collection

Together, these capabilities provide greater transparency and control over how carbon liabilities are valued and reflected in financial processes. For more information, check SAP Help: Carbon Certificate Management. Looking ahead, we will continue to further develop support in this area. While the current focus is on supporting the calculation and management of carbon liabilities, future developments will address the next phase of EU CBAM. Starting from 2027, companies will be required to purchase CBAM certificates to cover their reported emissions. To support this, SAP Green Ledger will be extended with dedicated certificate management capabilities.

 

Determining Mandatory Accounts for Carbon Certificates

To support CBAM-relevant journal entry postings, account determination for carbon certificates has been enhanced with the Determine Mandatory Accounts app (formerly Manage Carbon-Only Accounts). It enables you to assign specific CBAM accounts to charts of accounts and company codes, supporting transactions such as liability recognition and fair value adjustments. This setup is mandatory for organizations subject to EU CBAM requirements. The app provides predefined CBAM account types for assets, liabilities, and operating expenses, which are typically maintained in a connected finance system and used in SAP Green Ledger. Postings to these accounts are generated automatically, ensuring alignment with underlying carbon processes.

Accounts Assignment for EU CBAM Accounts

Check out SAP Help for more information: Assign Carbon Certificate Accounts.

 

Linking Carbon Data to Projects

Carbon data can now be linked to projects and WBS (work breakdown structure) elements. A new WBS element field is available across the apps Manage Journal Entries, Display Line Items, Display Account Balances, and the Carbon Data Dashboard. This allows you to link carbon emissions directly to specific WBS elements. For that, project and WBS master data can now be imported from a connected finance system using our master data import capabilities and viewed in the new Display Projects app. This enhancement enables you to gain a more structured and detailed view of carbon data and makes it possible to analyze emissions at the level of individual projects and WBS elements.

Example Project in the Display Projects App

 

Supporting Multiple SAP Sustainability Footprint Management Systems

Another improvement is the increased flexibility in system integration with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management systems. Before, you could only connect one business system with the role SAP Sustainability Footprint Management to a SAP Green Ledger system. Now it is possible to create multiple business systems with that role. This allows you to work with more than one SAP Sustainability Footprint Management system, for example when importing carbon data from different sources. At the same time, each finance business system can be associated with only one SAP Sustainability Footprint Management system connected to a SAP Green Ledger system. This clear assignment ensures consistent and well-structured data integration. The setup is handled through business system configuration, where you define the system, assign its role, and maintain integration details such as the connected finance system.

 

Posting on Blocked Accounts

Posting behaviour has been made more flexible in SAP Green Ledger. Carbon collections can now be posted on accounts even if they are currently marked as blocked in the system. This is particularly relevant in scenarios where the account status may differ between SAP Green Ledger and the connected finance system. This can occur because account data in SAP Green Ledger is not updated in real time but is instead based on data imports from the finance system. As a result, the status in SAP Green Ledger may not always reflect the most current state.

Warning Message when Posting on a Blocked Account

Against this background, postings can still be carried out even if an account is indicated as blocked in SAP Green Ledger at a given point in time. Before posting, a warning message informs you if the account is marked as blocked. To ensure accuracy, you can verify the actual status of the account directly in the connected finance business system. Overall, this adjustment allows you to continue processes without interruption while still maintaining transparency and control over account usage.

 

Reversing Manual Journal Entries

You can now reverse manual journal entries in our Manage Journal Entries app, which makes it easier to correct postings and maintain accurate carbon data records. Reversing journal entries is possible if certain conditions are met, such as having the appropriate source type and not having been reversed previously. Once reversed, the system automatically creates corresponding journal entries in which debits and credits are swapped, ensuring that the financial impact is fully offset while preserving traceability.

The reversal information, including status, who initiated the reversal and when as well as the reversed journal entries, is available in the journal entry document, allowing you to track changes and maintain compliance with accounting standards. This enhancement helps ensure data accuracy while supporting controlled correction processes within SAP Green Ledger. For more details, have a look at SAP Help: Reverse Journal Entries.

 

Further Updates and Improvements

In addition to the key capabilities described above, several additional enhancements have been introduced to improve usability and transparency:

  • A new Journal Entry Source Type field is now available in key apps such as Manage Journal Entries, Display Line Items, and Display Account Balances and provides visibility into how journal entries were created (e.g., through allocation processes, liability valuation, carbon collection postings, imports, or manual entries and reversals).
  • Updates to the allocation functionality include the renaming of the allocation type Manual Allocation to Periodic Allocation and related UI adjustments.
  • Certain allocation options have been streamlined, such as the removal of the allocation type Account Balance Allocation from the Display Account Balances app.
  • External commenting is now available in the User Guide, enabling direct feedback and collaboration, see also Blog Post.

 

Additional Information

If you want to dive deeper into SAP Green Ledger, the SAP Help Portal is a good place to start. It provides detailed information on concepts, configuration, and application usage. You can also check the What’s New section, which is updated with every release and gives a complete overview of the latest additions. To see what's coming next, the SAP Road Map Explorer offers insight into planned features and upcoming innovations. It is updated regularly and outlines how the solution will continue to evolve.

entry.title: Mastering Product Carbon Footprints: New Learning Journey for SAP Sustainability Footprint Managemen
entry.content:
In today’s business landscape, moving beyond high-level corporate reporting is no longer optional. To drive real decarbonization, we must understand the emissions embedded in every single product—from raw material extraction to the factory gate.

With SAP Sustainability Footprint Management (SFM), companies can automate these complex calculations and gain precise insights into the environmental impact of their entire product portfolio.

Why Product Carbon Footprint (PCF)?

While a Corporate Carbon Footprint (CCF) provides an overview of an organization’s total emissions, the PCF breaks this data down to the product level. This granularity allows businesses to:

  • Identify emission hotspots within the supply chain or specific manufacturing steps.

  • Make data-driven decisions during product design and material sourcing.

  • Ensure transparency for customers, partners, and regulatory bodies.

What You Will Learn in This Learning Journey

This specialized course builds on the fundamentals of carbon accounting and dives deep into the world of product-related emission calculations. The focus is on integrating business data and applying rigorous calculation models.

Key Highlights of the Training:

  • Bridging CCF and PCF: Understand the relationship between corporate and product footprints and how to allocate emissions across your product lines.

  • Master Data Management: Learn to integrate relevant data from ERP systems, define organizational boundaries, and set up calculation variants.

  • Energy Flow Modeling: Use energy flow models to assign Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions directly to the resources used in the manufacturing process.

  • Cradle-to-Gate Calculation: Capture emissions across the entire lifecycle—from raw material purchase and transport to the finished product.

  • Scalability at Core: Discover how SAP SFM handles millions of products and complex Bills of Materials (BOMs) to provide automated, audit-ready results.

Who Is This Course For?

This Learning Journey is designed for:

  • Sustainability Leads and Consultants looking to implement PCF strategies.

  • Data Analysts tasked with connecting sustainability metrics with operational ERP data.

  • Product Managers aiming to optimize the environmental footprint of their product range.

Start Your Learning Journey Today!

Are you ready to take the next step toward a transparent and sustainable value chain?

👉 Access the Learning Journey: Establishing the Product Carbon Footprint with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management

Join the SAP Learning Group to connect with fellow learners and get your questions answered by experts. Let’s make sustainability measurable together!

Did you find this overview helpful? Have you started working with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management yet? Share your thoughts and experiences in the comments below!

entry.title: How to Establish a Multi-Impact Environmental Footprint with SAP
entry.content:
Why Multi-Impact Footprint Matters

Focusing only on carbon emissions provides an incomplete picture of environmental impact. Organizations increasingly need to consider additional factors such as water usage, resource depletion, and other environmental indicators.

SAP Sustainability Footprint Management enables companies to go beyond carbon and calculate multi-impact environmental footprints, offering a more holistic view of sustainability performance.

With multi-impact insights, businesses can:

  • Assess environmental impact across multiple categories
  • Identify hidden hotspots beyond carbon emissions
  • Make more informed sustainability decisions
  • Strengthen overall environmental performance

What You’ll Learn in This Course

This focused, intermediate-level course introduces the key concepts and calculations for multi-impact footprints:

  1. Understanding Multi-Impact Categories
    Learn the fundamentals of different environmental impact categories and how they complement carbon footprint analysis.
  2. Calculating Multi-Impact Footprints
    Discover how to calculate and analyze environmental impacts across multiple categories using SAP Sustainability Footprint Management.

You’ll also learn how to identify hotspots and derive insights to support targeted improvement initiatives.

Who Should Take the Course?

This learning journey is ideal for:

  • Business Users
  • Consultants

Prerequisites:

  • Establishing Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with Simple Configuration
  • Establishing Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with Advanced Configuration
  • Establishing the Product Carbon Footprint with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management

Go beyond carbon and gain a complete environmental perspective and explore the Course “Establishing the Multi-impact Environmental Footprint with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management” here.

entry.title: Beyond the Basics: Top 10 Best Practices for Advanced Carbon Accounting in 2026
entry.content:
Top 10 Best Practices for Carbon & Environmental Accounting

  1. Transition from "Estimated" to "Actual" Data: Move away from spend-based averages. Use primary activity data (e.g., actual material weights and energy consumption) directly from your ERP to ensure precision.

  2. Adopt the "Green Ledger" Mindset: Treat carbon like cash. Integrate CO2e into your financial chart of accounts to ensure double-entry rigor and auditability.

  3. Ensure 95% Scope 3 Coverage: In line with 2026 GHG Protocol updates, ensure you aren't just reporting the "easy" categories. Identify and fill data gaps in Category 1 (Purchased Goods) and Category 11 (Use of Sold Products).

  4. Avoid the "Carbon Tunnel Vision": Implement Multi-Impact assessments. Monitor water scarcity, land use, and acidification alongside CO2 to prevent "burden shifting" during product optimization.

  5. Automate ERP Integration: Eliminate manual spreadsheets. Use SAP Sustainability Footprint Management to automatically link emission factors to your material masters and production orders.

  6. Direct Supplier Data Exchange: Stop guessing your Scope 3. Use the SAP Sustainability Data Exchange to receive actual Product Carbon Footprint (PCF) data from suppliers via standardized protocols.

  7. Granular Scope 2 Reporting: For electricity, move toward matching consumption with renewable energy certificates on an hourly and regional basis to reflect true grid impact.

  8. AI-Powered Factor Mapping: Leverage AI to map thousands of ERP material lines to scientific emission databases (like ecoinvent), reducing manual effort and human error.

  9. Predictive Footprinting: Don’t just report the past. Use "What-if" simulations to see how changing a raw material or a logistics route affects the final product footprint before the decision is made.

  10. Establish a Digital Audit Trail: Ensure every gram of CO2 can be traced back to its source. A "Single Source of Truth" in the SAP Sustainability Control Tower is essential for CSRD compliance.

#SAP #Sustainability #CarbonAccounting #ESG #GreenLedger #DigitalTransformation #LearningJourney

entry.title: New “Sustainable Finance” Category for SAP Green Ledger on SAP Continuous Influence
entry.content:
We are happy to announce a new category for Sustainable Finance on SAP Continuous Influence Program (CIP).

The category is dedicated to SAP Green Ledger scenarios with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and is now available in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Finance Session.

With this new category, you can now submit Customer Influence requests for SAP Green Ledger related topics and vote for existing requests directly in the portal. This gives you the opportunity to influence future product enhancements and helps SAP better understand and prioritize requirements in the area of Sustainable Finance. In addition, the SAP CIP provides transparency into submitted requests and their status, allowing you to track which requests are under review, planned, or already delivered.

We look forward to shaping the future of SAP Green Ledger together with you!

You can now find the new “Sustainable Finance” category on SAP CIP for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Finance

entry.title: How to Understand SAP Green Ledger in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Why SAP Green Ledger Matters

Sustainability and financial data are often managed separately and making it difficult to align environmental impact with business performance.

SAP Green Ledger bridges this gap by integrating carbon data directly into financial accounting processes within SAP S/4HANA. This allows organizations to combine financial and environmental insights in one unified view.

With SAP Green Ledger, businesses can:

  • Align carbon emissions with financial data
  • Increase transparency for reporting and decision-making
  • Support carbon accounting alongside financial accounting
  • Enable better planning and analysis of sustainability performance

What You’ll Learn in This Course

This short, 45-minute course introduces the fundamentals of SAP Green Ledger:

  1. Financial Integration
    Understand how SAP Green Ledger relates to and integrates with financial accounting in SAP S/4HANA.
  2. Key Features and Capabilities
    Explore core functionalities, including integration, analytics, and planning capabilities.
  3. Business Value
    Learn how combining financial and sustainability data supports more informed and strategic decision-making.

Who Should Take the Course?

This course is ideal for:

  • Business Users working with finance and sustainability data
  • Consultants supporting SAP S/4HANA and sustainability solutions

No prerequisites are required, making it a great starting point for beginners.

Conclusion

Bridge the gap between sustainability and finance and explore the course “Outlining Green Ledger in SAP S/4HANA” here.

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Control Tower: Q1-Q2 2026 Updates & Roadmap Highlights
entry.content:
Introduction

ESG reporting software is moving from an emerging category to a mission-critical business capability, driven by regulation, finance-led operating models, and a clear shift in buyer expectations. Even as regulatory scope evolves, demand remains strong: organizations still need to maintain or expand sustainability reporting, strengthen data integrity, and prepare for recurring disclosure requirements.

At the same time, manual and fragmented reporting models are no longer sustainable. Sustainability teams continue to face growing time burdens, computational complexity, and error risk when ESG data is collected across spreadsheets and disconnected processes. This is increasingly a finance-led concern as well: CFOs and finance leaders are becoming more influential in ESG funding decisions and are placing greater emphasis on the integrity of non-financial data.

AI is raising expectations even further. Business leaders increasingly expect reporting processes to become faster, more automated, and more intelligent. Still, sustainability AI can only create trusted business value when it is grounded in governed, traceable, enterprise-grade ESG data. Without this foundation, AI-assisted reporting can accelerate drafting, but it cannot reliably explain, evidence, or defend the data behind the disclosure.

This is where SAP Business AI becomes central to SAP Sustainability Control Tower. SAP’s approach is not AI added on top of disconnected sustainability data. It is AI embedded into business processes, supported by enterprise context, governed workflows, and traceable data from SAP and non-SAP systems. SAP Sustainability Control Tower is designed to support this journey by centralizing sustainability data and insights, helping organizations ensure compliant reporting, embed sustainability into core processes, and drive measurable progress across the enterprise.

These latest updates build on that foundation. They strengthen the data, integration, workflow, analytics, and reporting capabilities that make AI-assisted sustainability management credible in practice.

Here’s what’s new in SAP Sustainability Control Tower as of Q2 2026:

Expanded coverage of pre-delivered regulatory scope

To help organizations accelerate their sustainability reporting readiness, SAP Sustainability Control Tower continues to expand its SAP-provided ESG reporting content. These enhancements support customers by providing additional value for ESRS metric coverage across existing SAP-owned and custom metrics.

Recent master data enhancements include new and enhanced dimensions, measures, and data points. For dimensions, new and enhanced content includes gender attributes, human rights incident values, grievance mechanism recipient types, and severity of human rights incidents. For measures, new content includes human rights incidents, monetary sanctions due to human rights incidents, and enhanced naming for the count of governance body members.

SAP-provided metric coverage for ESRS data points has also been significantly expanded. As of April 2026, SAP Sustainability Control Tower includes 200+ quantitative metrics and 650+ qualitative metrics, supporting organizations with a broader set of content for ESRS data-point requirements. This includes quantitative coverage across areas such as climate change, pollution, water and marine resources, biodiversity and ecosystems, resource use and circular economy, own workforce, and business conduct. Qualitative coverage spans ESRS 2 general disclosures and topical standards across environmental, social, and governance topics. Documentation is available in Help for SAP Sustainability Control Tower.

That growing catalog gives customers a stronger starting point, while still leaving room to extend the model for company-specific or industry-specific reporting needs.

Data collection process enhancements

Governed ESG reporting depends on clear ownership, timing, and workflow. SAP Sustainability Control Tower supports form-based data collection for scoped measures and metrics, helping organizations define how data should be collected, when it should be collected, and who should provide and approve the submitted information.

With the data collection process, users can define data collection forms and set up data collection instances to trigger workflow-based data collection for selected items. They can assign processors for a given period and define approvers to review submitted data for approval or rejection.

Recent enhancements extend support to qualitative metrics in form-based data collection. Users can group qualitative metrics within a single form, and when new fiscal variants are activated, existing forms linked to inactive fiscal variants are automatically deactivated. In addition, table headers remain visible while scrolling, making larger data collection forms easier to use.

The qualitative metric data entry experience has also been redesigned. Users can select from all available metrics and add data at the high-level corporate summary level or at a more granular reporting structure level. Relevant details are available in the reference section on the data entry screen, helping users understand the context for the information they are entering.

The reporting line is now also available as an organizational boundary in data collection. Users can create a reporting scope using the reporting line organization type when setting up organizational boundaries, then run “check for updates” in the Configure Data Collection or Configure Data Entry app to add the new entries to the configurable items list.

SAP Sustainability Footprint Management integration

SAP Sustainability Control Tower also enhances integration with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management for greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions data. Customers can import GHG emissions data from SAP Sustainability Footprint Management into SAP Sustainability Control Tower while preserving native source granularity.

The integration helps eliminate the need for external data transformation and aggregation. Period type can be configured per integration scenario, with supported period types including monthly, quarterly, and yearly based on the active fiscal year variant. Data is ingested into SAP Sustainability Control Tower at the selected granularity, without forced transformation, and replicated to target systems using the configured period type.

Recent enhancements include a new UI control for period granularity selection and configurable period selection, including single-period and period-range options. Fiscal year variant support also helps enable more accurate period mapping.

The same principle of flexibility applies to the broader ESG data model, where customers often need to adapt standard content to their own structures and reporting practices.

ESG Data Foundation extensibility

As sustainability reporting requirements evolve, organizations need flexibility to adapt data models, extend content, and transport configuration across environments. SAP Sustainability Control Tower provides an open, extensible ESG Data Foundation that supports both standard SAP content and customer-specific extensions.

Customers can add custom measures across Data Provider Interfaces and add up to 20 custom dimensions for SAP-provided or custom measures. The content lifecycle for extension artifacts supports custom measures, dimensions, and metrics, with export and import via JSON file format.

Recent enhancements improve the content lifecycle for extension artifacts. Export and import are available in the Manage Metrics, Manage Measures, and Manage Custom Dimensions apps. Export selection can be based on naming conventions and includes dependent custom measures and custom dimensions to help ensure consistency.

Import supports consistency in the target tenant during object creation, and can now also update existing extensibility artifacts.

This makes extension management more practical across systems and tenants, especially as ESG reporting models continue to evolve.

SAP Datasphere content coverage

SAP Sustainability Control Tower also keeps enhancing its integration with SAP Datasphere. Users can leverage SAP Datasphere integration to load actual data into the Manage Quantitative ESG Data app and to upload master data for reporting structures, such as business locations, legal entities, and reporting lines, into the Manage Master Data app.

The SAP Datasphere content package supports multiple Data Provider Interfaces, including accounting, corruption incidents, emissions, generic measures, general measures, injury, land site, people, resource use, water withdrawal, waste, energy, and removal reversal carbon credit. Recent enhancements include updates for emissions and people DPIs, as well as new content for removal reversal and carbon credit scenarios.

The next step is to make those connected data flows easier to apply in regulation-specific scenarios, such as the EU Taxonomy.

EU Taxonomy data integration enhancements

For EU Taxonomy reporting, SAP Sustainability Control Tower now offers enhanced capabilities to manage master data and measures data in a more integrated way.

In the Manage Master Data app, users can configure and manage master data and EU Taxonomy filters in one place. This includes managing company code values used by EU Taxonomy in the legal entity dimension, defining filters applied to SAP S/4HANA master data and activity data, importing master data and hierarchies from files, and importing master data and hierarchies from SAP S/4HANA with the SAP Data Framework.

 

In the Manage Quantitative ESG Data app, users can import EU Taxonomy measures data, including activity data and denominator data. The integration supports imports from files and SAP S/4HANA, with validation rules to check consistency against master data and filters defined in the Manage Master Data app.
